title: Decades-old weeping willow collapses in Boston Public Garden
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 68-year-old weeping willow collapsed in Boston Public Garden, surpassing its typical lifespan of 30-50 years.
Friends of the Public Garden regularly inspect and maintain trees, finding the willow structurally sound before its fall.
No injuries occurred from the collapse; another diseased willow was removed from the garden earlier in January.

title: These new speed boards in Jamaica Plain, on the Esplanade track cyclists’ speeds, not just cars
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: New speed boards in Jamaica Plain and Esplanade target cyclists and drivers to reduce high speeds, according to DCR.
Speed boards gather data on traffic volume to help assess activity levels in areas like the Southwest Corridor Park.
Installations on paths such as the Cape Cod Rail Trail focus solely on monitoring cyclist speeds, not vehicles.

title: Thousands demand Wu address street safety after cyclist killed in crash
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 4,000 people signed an open letter urging Mayor Michelle Wu to improve street safety after Louisa Gag's death.
The letter criticizes stalled safety projects and calls for a permanent chief of streets and restored funding.
Mayor Wu announced increased enforcement and staffing to accelerate street safety improvements after Gag's fatal crash.

title: Police investigating after man, woman in their 70s found dead in Maine home
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Maine State Police are investigating after Carolyn Harris, 77, and Kevin Harris, 75, were found dead in their West Bath home.
The Sagadahoc County Sheriff's Department discovered the bodies during a welfare check on State Road, West Bath, July 13.
Autopsies are underway to determine the cause of death; police state there is no public danger.

title: Multiple issues after getting an SUV back from the body shop
author: John Paul
bullets: A reader's 2024 Honda CR-V EX has sensor and light issues after front-end repairs, indicating a potential ADAS recalibration need.
Vehicle features like auto-on headlights may require programming checks to restore functionality to pre-crash condition.
Contacting the insurance company is recommended to possibly open a supplemental claim for unresolved repair issues.

title: Officers shot at during Dorchester traffic stop, police say, prompting outcry from union
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two Boston police officers were shot at during a traffic stop in Dorchester; they escaped without injury, police say.
Rasiel Joel Carbuccia, 20, was charged with multiple offenses, including assault and battery with a dangerous weapon.
The Boston Police Patrolmen's Association urged officials to condemn the attack and emphasized officers' rights to safety.

title: Woman, 25, dead, driver and 3 children hospitalized in crash on I-495 in Andover
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 25-year-old woman from Lawrence died in a crash on I-495 North near Route 93 in Andover.
The driver of the minivan was hospitalized with serious injuries, while three children had unknown injuries.
The crash, involving a minivan and a sedan, is under investigation by State Police.

title: 3 longtime tenants to close or move after Alpha Management buys prominent Allston commercial building
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Alpha Management purchased a commercial building at 149-175 Harvard Ave., displacing three longtime Allston businesses.
The Pet Shop, Ritual Arts, and Regeneration Tattoo are closing or relocating due to increased rents from new ownership.
Alpha Management plans renovations but faces concerns about affecting Allston's cultural and commercial identity.

title: Paul Pierce, Kevin Garnett to introduce Doc Rivers at Hall of Fame ceremony
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Paul Pierce and Kevin Garnett will present Doc Rivers at the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame ceremony.
Doc Rivers will be inducted as a coach with 1,194 career wins, sixth all-time among NBA coaches.
The Hall of Fame ceremony is scheduled for August 15 at Symphony Hall in Springfield.

title: Jayson Tatum says it’s ‘weird’ that Jaylen Brown is no longer a Celtic
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum expressed that it's 'weird' to no longer have Jaylen Brown as a teammate after Brown's trade to the 76ers.
Jaylen Brown was traded by the Celtics to the 76ers for Paul George, two first-round picks, and two second-round picks.
The trade sparked emotional responses from Celtics fans, including a viral reaction from a young fan comforted by Brown.
